meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Magnitude 7.5 earthquake strikes northern Japan; tsunami warning issued
items.summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi says the government has set up a crisis management team following the strong earthquake.

items.title: Ukraine claims attack on Russian warships in occupied Crimea
items.summary: Ukraine's military intelligence says two large landing ships struck in Sevastopol Bay in Russian-occupied Crimea.

items.title: Inside war-hit Sudan’s only functioning hospital curing tropical diseases
items.summary: Sudan's health ministry says 37 percent of country's health facilities are out of service as war enters its fourth year.

items.title: Fans fight police at Paraguay football match
items.summary: Fans fight police at Paraguay football match

items.title: Palestinian children protest against siege on their school
items.summary: Palestinian children protested against Israeli settlers and troops blocking the only safe route to their school.

items.title: The Strokes highlight destroyed Gaza and Iran universities at Coachella
items.summary: US band The Strokes used their Coachella set to showcase the US-Israeli destruction of universities in Gaza and Iran.

items.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 52 of US-Israeli attacks?
items.summary: Islamabad talks in limbo as Iran says it will retaliate after US marines capture an Iranian ship near Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Iran says no talks with US for now, casting doubt over Pakistan efforts
items.summary: Iran has said it will not be sending negotiators to Pakistan for talks with the US, amid rising tensions.

items.title: US military releases video of marines seizing Iranian ship
items.summary: Video from the US military show an operation by its forces to seize an Iranian-flagged ship near the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Wembanyama makes history as Spurs defeat Blazers in Game 1
items.summary: Victor Wembanyama set a new San Antonio record for the most points in a playoff debut as the Spurs outlast Portland.

items.title: Kane scores as Bayern Munich claim Bundesliga title with Stuttgart victory
items.summary: Harry Kane helps Munich wrap up a second straight Germany premier division crown with a month still to go in the season.

items.title: As barbed wire blocks kids from class, Palestinians stage ‘Freedom School’
items.summary: Children from Umm al-Khair in the occupied West Bank protest after settlers erect a fence blocking access to school.

items.title: To stay or to go? No good options for Lebanon’s displaced
items.summary: Residents of southern Lebanon face a stark choice: stay home and risk being killed, or flee and risk deepening poverty.

items.title: What is really happening in northern Nigeria
items.summary: There is no US-led conspiracy or a ‘Christian genocide’. There is an insurgency that has adapted.

items.title: Outrage after Israeli soldier desecrates statue of Jesus Christ in Lebanon
items.summary: A photo of an Israeli soldier smashing a statue of Jesus Christ in southern Lebanon has sparked widespread outrage.

items.title: Oil prices surge amid mixed signals on US-Iran peace talks
items.summary: Brent crude rises more than 7 percent as Washington and Tehran offer conflicting accounts on ceasefire negotiations.

items.title: What’s behind the US army’s decision to raise enlistment age to 42?
items.summary: Changes in US Army enlistment standards could be an effort to boost recruitment as numbers have lagged in recent years.

items.title: Iran war live: Tehran will skip talks as tensions rise over US ship seizure
items.summary: Attack comes hours after US President Donald Trump announced he is sending his team to Islamabad for possible talks.

items.title: Outrage after photo shows Israeli soldier smashing Jesus statue in Lebanon
items.summary: Social media users condemn Western silence on attacks on religious symbols and sites by Israeli soldiers and settlers.

items.title: US forces attack and seize Iranian ship Touska near Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: US Central Command has published a video said to show a guided-missile destroyer firing at an Iranian-flagged ship.

items.title: Argentina’s Milei backs US-Israel war on Iran in Jerusalem visit
items.summary: Argentina’s president reaffirmed alignment with the US and Israel during a visit to Jerusalem, backing their war on Iran

items.title: Schools, shops shut in northern Israel to protest the Lebanon ceasefire
items.summary: Shops and schools shut in northern Israel as residents protested a 10-day ceasefire with Lebanon.

items.title: Protesters defy Berlin rain to call for end to global wars
items.summary: Hundreds protested in Berlin against the US-Israeli aggression on Iran, and Israeli attacks on Gaza and Lebanon.

items.title: Who will shape the global agenda, the left or far right?
items.summary: Left-wing leaders meet in Barcelona, while far-right protesters march in Milan.

items.title: With loss at Man City, have Arsenal lost their grip on the Premier League?
items.summary: Arsenal's loss to City and a woeful end-of-season run have now cost them their once-comfortable, top-of-the-table lead.
